title: Agilent custom microarray data for Cyprinus carpio (common carp)
author: Pluta, Aneta
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Agilent custom microarray data for common carp (Cyprinus carpio) ================================================================ 
 Dataset purpose --------------- This dataset accompanies the manuscript *"Transcriptional profiling of common carp: a microarray-based framework for aquaculture research"* (Int. J. Mol. Sci., MDPI; in preparation). It provides raw Feature Extraction outputs, processed expression matrices, and platform annotation for a custom Agilent 8×60K array designed against the SPL01 reference genome. 
 Contents -------- - **Agilent custom microarray data for GEO_carp/** (zip root)   - **Raw data files/** — Agilent Feature Extraction `.txt` files (two-color protocol GE2_1200_Jun14).   - **Processed data file/** — normalized expression matrix for the healthy control set.   - **Metadata spreadsheet/** — sample/experiment metadata aligned with MIAME (GEO/ArrayExpress-compatible).   - **GEO_Agilent_custom_microarrray.xlsx** — platform and sample annotations (ProbeID/TargetID/GeneSymbol; study metadata). 
 Recommended citation -------------------- If you reuse the data, please cite: Pluta A., Paździor E., Fletcher D., Karwatowicz M. (2025). Agilent custom microarray data for *Cyprinus carpio* (common carp). Zenodo. https://doi.org/DOI_TBD 
 Versioning ---------- Zenodo assigns a DOI to the dataset and a concept DOI for all versions. Please cite the specific version you used. 
 Provenance & methods (short) ---------------------------- - Species: *Cyprinus carpio* (common carp); tissue: head kidney (pronephros), healthy controls. - RNA integrity: RIN ≥ 9.0; two independent RNA isolations pooled as A/B. - Labeling: Agilent Low Input Quick Amp, two-color workflow; spike-in A→Cy3, B→Cy5. - Hybridization: 65 °C, 17 h (8×60K), Gene Expression Wash Buffer kit. - Scanning: Agilent G2505C at 3 µm; Feature Extraction v12.0.3.1 (GE2_1200_Jun14). - Processing: FE background-corrected `gProcessedSignal`/`rProcessedSignal`; log2 transform; baseline-to-median centering; FE flags used to filter; technical replicates summarized to probe level. 
 File integrity -------------- A machine-readable checksum manifest (SHA256) is provided as `CHECKSUMS_SHA256.txt` for all files within the supplied zip archive. 
 Licensing --------- **CC BY 4.0**. You are free to share and adapt the material with attribution to the authors and the National Veterinary Research Institute (NVRI). 
 Contact ------- Corresponding: Aneta Pluta — aneta.pluta@piwet.pulawy.pl Secondary: Ewa Paździor — ewa.pazdzior@piwet.pulawy.pl
